generated.findings: Right upper lobe opacity, left lower lung atelectasis, eventration of the right hemidiaphragm. No focal lung consolidation, pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
generated.impression: The findings are consistent with a possible right upper lobe pneumonia, but due to low agreement score (0.40) for the top-voted label 'Lung Opacity', there is clinical uncertainty.
generated.recommendation: Further evaluation recommended to determine extent of disease and guide management.
generated.disclaimer: Clinical uncertainty exists due to low agreement score (0.40) for the top-voted label 'Lung Opacity'.

ground_truth.findings: Frontal and lateral views of the chest show an unchanged cardiomediastinal silhouette. There is [REDACTED] airspace opacity in the mid right lung radiating from the right hilum to the pleura and bordered inferiorly by the fissures. The [REDACTED] fissure is convex upward. There is right base patchy airspace opacity. This appears chronic and may be due to scarring. There is no significant pleural effusion.
ground_truth.impression: Right upper lobe airspace consolidation Please correlate clinically for pneumonia.
